JavaScript tests for Number object prototype properties and behaviors.


## Files
- **[15.07.03.01-001.js](15.07.03.01-001.js.md)**: Tests if the Number object has a "prototype" property.
- **[15.07.03.01-002.js](15.07.03.01-002.js.md)**: Tests the property descriptors of `Number.prototype` for writability, enumerability, and configurability.
- **[15.07.03.01-003.js](15.07.03.01-003.js.md)**: Tests if the prototype of a new Number object is Number.prototype.
- **[15.07.03.01-004.js](15.07.03.01-004.js.md)**: Tests the immutability of the Number.prototype object in JavaScript.
- **[15.07.03.01-005.js](15.07.03.01-005.js.md)**: Tests the immutability of the Number prototype by asserting that it cannot be deleted.
- **[15.07.03.01-006.js](15.07.03.01-006.js.md)**: Tests if the 'prototype' property of Number is not enumerable.
- **[15.07.03.01-007.js](15.07.03.01-007.js.md)**: Tests that the "prototype" property does not exist in the Number object.
- **[15.07.03.01-008.js](15.07.03.01-008.js.md)**: Tests the behavior of `Number.prototype.toString` after deletion in JavaScript.
- **[15.07.03.01-009.js](15.07.03.01-009.js.md)**: Tests the `toString` method of `Number.prototype` by assigning it from `Object.prototype.toString`.
- **[15.07.03.01-010.js](15.07.03.01-010.js.md)**: Tests if the prototype of Number is equal to +0.
- **[15.07.03.01-011.js](15.07.03.01-011.js.md)**: Tests if dividing 1 by `Number.prototype` results in `Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY`.
